black_swan.title: Black Swan Events
black_swan.enable_desc: GM may inject unexpected world events to break narrative predictability (sprint 5 Black Swan agent). Disable for a calm, predictable narrative.

extractor.title: Narrative Extractor (GM Step 2)
extractor.enable_desc: Split GM into two steps: main model for narrative, cheaper model for structured ops. ~5x lower error rate, ~20% higher cost.
extractor.api_desc: Anthropic uses native tool_use (most stable); Vertex uses response_mime_type=application/json; others use OpenAI-compatible response_format.
extractor.model_desc: Recommended: budget tiers of current flagship models — gemini-3.5-flash / claude-haiku-4 / gpt-5.5-nano / qwen-3.7-flash etc.

clarify.title: Curator Clarify Threshold
clarify.threshold_desc: When confidence is below this value, curator skips GM and asks the player directly. 0 = never ask, 1 = always ask first.

modelparams.title: Model Settings
modelparams.description: Sampling parameters · Applies to all API calls
modelparams.preset_desc: Quick-switch a set of common parameters; selecting Custom prevents overwrite.
modelparams.reasoning_desc: Thinking depth for reasoning models (o3 / R1 etc.); low = fast & token-efficient, high = deepest.
modelparams.max_tokens_desc: Maximum response length per turn; too short will cause truncation.
modelparams.context_size_desc: Upper limit of tokens per request; older history is truncated when exceeded.
modelparams.seed_desc: Same seed + same input = reproducible output; -1 = random each time.
modelparams.stop_desc: Stop generating when these strings appear; separate multiple with |.
modelparams.nsfw_desc: Controls whether GM generates sensitive content involving sex / violence. Sexualization of minors is always blocked regardless of mode.
modelparams.nsfw_intensity_desc: 0 = only on explicit player request; 1 = GM may actively escalate. Effective only in Open / Explicit mode.
modelparams.nsfw_extra_desc: Appended to system prompt; use for additional prohibitions, age verification, story prerequisites.
modelparams.mirostat_desc: Dynamic temperature adjustment; effective on some local models.
modelparams.preview_json_desc: Actual sampling parameters sent to the API.

modules.title: Per-Module Model Assignment
modules.description: Set your personal default main GM model, and optionally assign independent models per LLM submodule.
modules.hint: The main GM default is saved to your personal preferences. Other modules reuse it unless overridden. The list only shows provider models you have configured and synced.
modules.inherit_tip: Not overridden — using main GM's current model
modules.footer: Changes are saved immediately via POST /api/me/preference. Each module picks up the new setting on next invocation.

group_desc.core: The Three-Sage pipeline: Narrator prose, Planner orchestration, Recorder bookkeeping, plus core GM helper models.
group_desc.script: Models for script editing, character-card generation / import, and review.
group_desc.world: World progression: storyline anchors, phase digests, black-swan events.
group_desc.misc: Generic fallback model used when a module isn't configured separately.

ctx_tiered.label: Tiered context cache
ctx_tiered.desc: Splits the stable prefix from dynamic content to hit the model's prefix cache — significant token savings.

recorder_unified.label: Unified Recorder
recorder_unified.desc: Merges state extraction and storyline-anchor reconciliation into a single LLM call.

narrator_slim.label: Slim Narrator (no tool loop)
narrator_slim.desc: Main narration is written in one pass without the tool loop (the biggest token multiplier); state writes go to the Recorder.

rag_gate.label: RAG retrieval gate
rag_gate.desc: The Planner decides whether this turn needs source retrieval; skips it when not, saving retrieval tokens.

anchor_pace.label: Storyline anchor pacing
anchor_pace.desc: Advances anchors at the conversation's real pace, marks same-chapter anchors one by one, and invalidates anchors when a character dies — fixes chapter-skipping / off-anchor drift.

kb_state.label: DB-resident save knowledge base
kb_state.desc: Stores save state as database rows (single source of truth) for precise retrieval and maintenance; off uses the traditional whole-save blob.

memory.recall_depth_desc: Max passages retrieved from source per turn (2–20).
memory.summary_window_desc: Compress the last N turns into a summary for injection (3–20).
memory.token_budget_desc: Max tokens of memory content injected into context per request (200–2000).
memory.auto_archive: Auto-archive Old Memories After N Turns
memory.auto_archive_desc: Memory entries older than this many turns are auto-archived to the fact store (10–200).
memory.pinned_max_desc: Max entries in the pinned bucket (5–100). Older entries are moved to fact store when exceeded.
memory.bucket_pinned_desc: When disabled, pinned bucket is not maintained; recall only uses time-based and character buckets.
memory.bucket_world_desc: When disabled, world-lore memory retrieval injection is skipped.
memory.bucket_character_desc: When disabled, character-relationship memory retrieval injection is skipped.

permissions.title: Write Permissions
permissions.default_mode_desc: Default permissions used when creating a new save. Can be changed in-game at any time.
permissions.mode_review: Auto Review — low-risk pass, high-risk prompt
permissions.mode_full: Full Access — prompt only for major world-line changes
permissions.high_risk_desc: Even in Full Access mode, these fields trigger a non-blocking confirmation. Click to toggle.
permissions.custom_whitelist_desc: Add custom paths (e.g. world.weather.severity); these always prompt even in Full Access mode.
permissions.err_invalid: Only letters, digits, underscores, dots, and asterisks are allowed; must start with a letter or underscore
permissions.err_in_builtin: This path is already in the built-in whitelist
permissions.audit_log_desc: Last 200 state write / rejection / parse-failure records (per save). Empty when no active save.
permissions.audit_no_state: No active save. Audit entries generated during gameplay will appear here.

deploy.title: Deployment
deploy.warning: Note: Network-level settings such as listen address and CORS origins require a service restart to take effect. SMTP and CAPTCHA credentials take effect immediately.
deploy.listen_addr_desc: Use 127.0.0.1 for local-only access. Requires restart.
deploy.cors_desc: Comma-separated; use * to allow all. Requires restart.
deploy.upload_limit_desc: Maximum single file size, e.g. 12MB or 1GB. Requires restart.
deploy.upload_limit_error: Invalid format. Enter something like 12MB, 512KB, or 1GB
deploy.smtp_desc: Used for registration verification, password reset, and subscription notifications. Disabled means local placeholder mail.
deploy.smtp_preset_desc: Quick-fill common provider settings; you can fine-tune after selecting.
deploy.smtp_from_desc: Sender shown to recipients; recommended to use a domain email.
deploy.smtp_test_desc: Send a test email to the current account's email address immediately.
deploy.captcha_desc: Used for registration / password reset / login retry. Recommended in production.
deploy.captcha_recaptcha_ver_desc: v2 = popup challenge · v3 = invisible scoring; v3 recommended.
deploy.captcha_score_desc: Scores below this are treated as bots; 0.5 is a good starting point.
deploy.captcha_widget_desc: Non-interactive suits most cases; interactive adds a challenge for suspicious users.
deploy.captcha_triggers_desc: Check the features that require verification; forced after 3 failed logins by default.

welcome.title: Welcome to Beta Platform
welcome.beta_warning_body: This platform is in **closed beta** and may have critical bugs. Data loss is possible — do not rely solely on this platform for important content.

welcome.api_section_body: Both the GM chat and the RAG embedder are bring-your-own-key (BYOK): you provide and configure your own AI API Key (Anthropic / OpenAI / Vertex / DeepSeek, etc.). The platform does not provide AI API calls on your behalf.

announce.title: Story Experience Upgrade
announce.line_1: Closer to the source: the GM follows the original characters, setting, and plot more faithfully, with less off-track improvisation.
announce.line_2: No spoilers: characters and key events from chapters you haven't reached yet won't be revealed early.
announce.line_3: More accurate progress: story progress follows where you've actually played, with no unexplained chapter jumps.
announce.note: Existing saves are unaffected and continue as usual; to try the new system, just start a new game.

import.preview_footer: Showing first {{count}} chapters · See full split in the script directory after import · POST /api/v1/scripts/import
import.basic_desc: Give the script a name and choose a chapter split rule.
import.field_title_desc: Leave blank to use the filename
import.field_custom_regex_desc: Only applies when 'Custom' rule is selected
import.pipeline_options_desc: Pipeline has 8 stages: ① chunks → ② chapter facts → ③ entity extract → ④ NPC cards (LLM) → ⑤ worldbook (LLM) → ⑥ canon extract (LLM) → ⑦ timeline anchors → ⑧ embeddings. Only stages ④/⑤ are toggleable — the others are core (chunks/facts/canon/index always run). Uncheck both to skip LLM entirely (chapter index + embeddings only).
import.enable_cards_desc: Scans first 30 chapters for frequent characters and writes editable cards (LLM call).
import.enable_worldbook_desc: Aggregates entities across all chapters into worldbook entries (LLM call).
import.partial_pipeline_warn: ⚠ Disabling either step means you get chapter index + vectors only; the GM will lack characters/world context at chat time. You can backfill later from the Knowledge Extract panel.
import.file_desc: Drop or select a TXT / MD file, max 50 MB.
import.warn_no_file: Please select a local script file before estimating.
import.preview_analyzing: Backend is splitting and counting chapters…
import.preview_invalidated_detail: Preview the chapter split again before importing so the budget and uploaded file state match the current rule.
import.import_busy: Import is already running. Please wait.
import.importing_bg: Import in progress — you can close the window and come back.

import.embedder_error_title: Embedding configuration may have an issue
import.embedder_not_configured_body:
The vector index needs a separately-configured embedding (RAG) model — this is distinct from the main chat LLM key. Without it the vector index cannot be built and RAG semantic recall degrades to keyword matching.
Note: not every provider offers embeddings — Anthropic and DeepSeek do not. Configure an API key that supports the /embeddings endpoint (e.g. OpenAI, Qwen/DashScope, SiliconFlow, Cohere, Vertex) under Settings → RAG / Embedding model.
import.embedder_not_configured_title: No RAG / embedding model configured (optional, but recommended)

review.extract_done_detail: Script has been reset to 'Pending Review'
review.cancel_requested: Cancel requested (takes effect at next checkpoint)
review.must_estimate_first: To prevent accidental token burn, you must click 'Estimate Cost' first. Changing any parameter requires a fresh estimate.
review.scope_desc: Default 'Full' re-imports the whole script. 'Embeddings only' just regenerates canon entity vectors — no LLM calls.
review.scope_embed_only_note: No LLM calls. Regenerates the vector index for existing knowledge base characters (use when changing the embedding model or backfilling missing vectors). Uses your own BYOK credentials (Vertex / other embedding provider) — not platform-paid.
review.scope_worldbook_only_note: No LLM calls. Rebuilds the worldbook from existing knowledge base characters (use after tweaking importance thresholds / subtype rendering). $0.
review.scope_anchors_only_note: No LLM calls. Rebuilds timeline anchors from chapter summary story-time labels (use after fixing aggregation logic or story_phase). $0.
review.chapter_range_desc: Re-extract only this chapter range (targeted redo). Empty = whole book.
review.desc: Re-run full-book LLM knowledge extraction (seed vocab → arc/per-chapter extraction → entity resolution → embedding). Runs in the background; you can close the window and check back later. The script will be marked as 'Pending Review' when done.
review.provider_empty: No APIs configured in Model Manager

identity.intro: The identity card is a positioning overlay on top of the character card (undercover / exiled noble / shelter refugee …) — unrelated to name or appearance, independent from the player origin setting below. Optional — leave blank to play as the selected character directly.
identity.no_identity: No identity card — will play as "{{name}}" directly
identity.no_identity_required: No identity card is attached. The save will start directly from the selected character.
identity.npc_desc: Pick a canon character as the protagonist's forgotten true identity. This doesn't conflict with that character card — the protagonist simply has amnesia; when they remember is up to your in-game choices.
identity.ai_desc: Based on script + starting point + selected character card — generates 4 distinct identities.
identity.ai_desc_soul: Generate 4 candidates where a modern-era soul transmigrates into a local body. Skip to use the bare soul-traveler overlay (original character + modern memories) only.
identity.ai_desc_body: Generate 4 candidates for your whole self (body + soul) having crossed into this world as a complete outsider — no local identity card.
identity.ai_desc_dual: Generate 4 dual-soul scenario candidates where your soul and the native soul share one body. The GM will voice the native soul.
identity.ai_desc_native: Generate 4 candidates native to the source world (refugee / undercover / exiled noble …), no modern memories. Skip to keep the original character identity.
identity.ai_desc_isekai: Generate 4 candidates where a modern-era soul has transmigrated into someone in this world. Skip to use the bare time-traveler overlay (original character + modern memories) only.
identity.origin_section_hint: Determines how you enter this story world, affecting how the GM handles your memories, knowledge, and soul configuration.
identity.id_section_hint: The protagonist's social identity in the world, layered on top of the character card. Skip to start as the chosen card "{{name}}" directly.
identity.id_section_hint_soul: Identity = the social standing of the local body you entered (name / ties / position).
identity.id_section_hint_body: A total outsider with no local identity. You start as "{{name}}".
identity.id_section_hint_dual: Identity = the native sharing your body; the GM plays their soul.
identity.id_section_hint_native: You are "{{name}}". The identity is just their social role (post / faction), not a different person.
identity.npc_empty: This script has no selectable canon character cards yet.
identity.npc_known_hint: When taking a canon character's identity, picking "amnesiac" usually makes for a stronger opening — the identity unfolds in play. You can also pick "aware".
identity.known_na_body: A full transfer brings your own body and memories into this world, so there is no local identity to be aware of.
identity.origin_soul_mapping: Character card → foreign soul (memories/knowledge)   Identity card → local body identity (social position/ties)
identity.origin_soul_hint: Classic isekai transmigration. Your mind is a modern person's, occupying the local character's body, with partial foreknowledge of the source story.
identity.origin_body_mapping: Character card → yourself (body + soul)   Identity card → none (complete outsider)
identity.origin_body_hint: Your whole self crosses over. No local identity card — you are a complete outsider with no pre-existing social ties in this world.
identity.origin_dual_essence: Your soul + native soul share one body
identity.origin_dual_mapping: Character card → your soul's perspective   Identity card → native original (GM voices the other soul)
identity.origin_dual_hint: Two consciousnesses share one body. The GM will roleplay the native soul from the identity card. Decisions emerge from collision between the two.
identity.origin_native_mapping: Character card → yourself   Identity card → your social position in this world
identity.origin_native_hint: No foreign soul — you are this character. The GM strictly enforces world-canon and will push back on actions that break the character's established nature.
identity.identity_known_hint: Only applies when an identity card is attached and origin ≠ Body Transfer. Affects your knowledge of that identity at game start.
identity.identity_known_true_desc: You know your identity from the start — social ties, secrets, and positioning are clear.
identity.identity_known_false_desc: You begin with amnesia or just waking up — your identity is discovered gradually through the story.
identity.ai_empty: No recommendations returned. Try manual creation or use the character card directly.
identity.field_alias_desc: Optional — leave blank to use the character card's name
identity.field_role_constraint: At least one of role or background required
identity.field_role_placeholder: e.g. Time-traveler princess / Undercover agent / Exiled noble
identity.field_bg_placeholder: Origin, motivation, and connection to key events in the story world
identity.origin_locked_banner: Playing Script NPC "{{name}}" — origin locked to Native (you are this character; GM strictly enforces the worldview).
identity.origin_locked_npc: Playing a script NPC — origin locked to Native

gm_style_editor.title: Narrative Style (Linear Sliders)
gm_style_editor.desc_script: Showing the effective style for this script (your personal defaults already applied). Only adjust the knobs you want to customize specifically for this script — unchanged knobs continue to inherit your personal or platform defaults.
gm_style_editor.desc_user: Your global default GM style, applied to all scripts that don't have their own override. Script-level or save-level settings take priority.
gm_style_editor.ok_saved_script: Saved. This script's style has been updated — takes effect from the next GM turn.
gm_style_editor.ok_saved_user: Saved. Your default style has been updated — takes effect from the next GM turn.
gm_style_editor.readonly_notice: Only the script author can edit this script's narrative style.

reply_length.label: Response Length
reply_length.desc: How long each GM turn is. Lower values produce shorter, tighter prose; higher values yield longer passages with more scene-setting and detail.

player_action_focus.label: Camera Focus
player_action_focus.desc: Who takes center stage. Lower values focus on the NPC and world reacting to you (your action is briefly acknowledged); higher values describe your action itself in rich detail.

drama_density.label: Drama Density
drama_density.desc: Lower values match the energy of your input (quiet in, quiet out); higher values push the GM to introduce conflict, reversals, and tension regardless of your tone.

interiority.label: Inner Life
interiority.desc: Lower values stick to visible actions, expressions, and dialogue; higher values add NPC inner thoughts, subtext, and unspoken motivations.

cliffhanger.label: Cliffhanger
cliffhanger.desc: Ending tension. Lower values wrap each turn with a sense of closure; higher values leave imminent danger or unresolved threads to pull you into the next turn.

guidance_force.label: Steering Strength
guidance_force.desc: Lower values let you freely improvise; higher values have the GM actively drive the plot toward the source material's key story beats.
